findings.evidence: head uses `customer_id=customer.id` (attribute of fetched Customer object) where base used `customer_id=customer_id` (the raw function parameter). In the current Repository implementation get_customer is a dict lookup keyed on customer_id, so customer.id == customer_id always. Functionally equivalent today, but a future Repository that normalises ids on write (e.g. lowercasing) would make the two differ silently.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: All test fixtures pass string literals like 'C1' that are stored and returned verbatim, so customer.id == customer_id in every exercised path. No test probes a caller-supplied id that differs in form from the stored id.
findings.fix: No action required for the current implementation. Worth a comment noting the equivalence assumption if the Repository contract is ever broadened.
